LAW AND MOTION 302, TASHJIAN'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED ANSWER IS GRANTED CONDITIONED ON DEFENDANT STIPULATING TO A TRIAL CONTINUANCE IN ANY LENGTH SOUGHT BY PLAINTIFF NOT EXCEEDING TEN MONTHS. DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN GOOD CAUSE FOR THE MOTION AND THE CONDITION UPON WHICH THE MOTION IS GRANTED REMOVES ANY PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF M SAMUELS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(H). MATTER IS CONTINUED TO JAN-19-2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 AND SUBJECT ONLY TO ANY ISSUE REGARDING CREDITS FOR PAYMENTS AND RATE INCREASES. PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION IS LIMITED ONLY TO THOSE ISSUES AND TO TAKE PLACE BY DECEMBER 17, 2011. SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION TO BE FILED BY JANUARY 4, 2012. SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY TO BE FILED BY JANUARY 11, 2012. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT RALPH TASHJIAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS DENIED. SANCTIONS OF $570 ($285 PER HOUR FOR TWO HOURS) TO BE PAID BY DEFENDANT TO PLAINTIFF NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 16, 2011. WHILE DEFENDANT HAS THE RIGHT TO TAKE ITS MOTION OFF CALENDAR, HE HAS NO RIGHT TO TAKE OFF CALENDAR PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. DEFENDANT'S MOTION LACKED SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION BECAUSE IT DID NOT INCLUDE A SEPARATE STATEMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Nov-18-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEVEN M. KIPPERMAN, TEL. NO. 397-8600 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT RALPH TASHJIAN'S Motion To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents IS
Denied. Sanctions of $570 ($285 per hour for two hours) to be paid by defendant to plaintiff no later than December 16, 2011. While defendant has the right to take its motion off calendar, he has no right to take off calendar plaintiff's request for sanctions. Defendant's motion lacked substantial justification because it did not include a separate statement. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
